title: AMW v2: Implement beads-aware wake gate in agent-mail-watcher
description:
Expand `AMW-v2.md` into an execution-ready issue graph for adding a beads-aware wake gate to `tools/agent-mail-watcher`.

Target behavior: urgent-only wakes when a project has zero open beads; high-and-urgent-only wakes when work exists but nothing is ready; otherwise preserve the current wake path.
design:
* Keep the logic watcher-owned rather than moving it into `ntm`.
* Resolve the target pane first, then read work state from that pane checkout when the watcher can prove it belongs to the same Git workspace as the canonical project.
* Use `br` as the source of truth for counts and invoke it in read-only observer mode so the watcher does not mutate `.beads`.
* Treat policy suppression as terminal (`delivered = true`) and preserve existing busy suppression, ownership checks, provider checks, and bootstrap/autocreate behavior outside the new policy gate.
* Make automated verification a first-class deliverable: unit tests for helper logic and invariants, self-test coverage for integrated watcher behavior, and scripted e2e scenarios with rich artifacts and diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria:
* Non-urgent wakes are blocked when `open_count == 0`.
* Low and normal wakes are blocked when `open_count > 0` and `ready_count == 0`.
* Urgent wake behavior still works after completion and high still works in the no-ready state.
* Flag-disabled operation preserves exact pre-v2 wake behavior.
* Status output and event logs expose enough state to explain every beads-gate decision.
* The implementation leaves existing ownership, provider, busy, and bootstrap behavior intact.
* Unit tests, self-test coverage, and scripted e2e validation all exist and provide actionable diagnostics on failure.
notes:
Background: `AMW-v2.md` already identifies four hazards that can make the implementation wrong even if the policy matrix looks simple: observer-mode `br` access, duplicate `br` calls, wrong repo-root selection, and ambiguous self-test seeding.

Revision rationale: the original bead graph covered the markdown plan well, but it still left three user-impacting gaps under-specified: exact feature-flag-disabled behavior, fast local unit-test coverage for low-level helpers and invariants, and scripted e2e verification with detailed logs/artifacts suitable for deployment confidence and debugging.

Structural choice: the numbered phase beads are now typed as epics instead of ordinary tasks. They are grouping nodes with their own design intent, not leaf work items. That should make the ready queue and human triage focus on actionable leaves rather than on summary nodes.

title: AMW v2 P1: Establish baseline and guardrails
description: Capture the starting state and freeze the execution guardrails before touching watcher logic.
design:
* Record current watcher behavior and the starting commit so later regressions have a baseline.
* Confirm the exact `br` observer-mode contract the patch will depend on.
* Make the goal, non-goals, and invariants explicit in the bead graph so implementation does not drift.
acceptance_criteria:
* Baseline watcher behavior and starting commit are captured.
* The observer-mode `br stats` contract is confirmed in a real beads repo.
* Later implementers can infer the allowed scope from the bead graph alone.
notes: Phase 1 now establishes four concrete guardrails for the remaining work: use pane-first workspace proof before reading work state, use read-only observer-mode `br` as the only count source, fail open when repo/DB resolution is missing or untrusted instead of trusting zero counts, and treat past-tense terminal recap text as idle history rather than active work.
close_reason: Completed: baseline capture, observer-mode br contract validation, and explicit guardrail/invariant language are all in place for later implementers.

title: AMW v2 P1.2: Validate the br observer-mode stats contract
description: Prove the exact `br` invocation the watcher will rely on: `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats`.
design:
* Confirm where `open_issues`, `ready_issues`, and `in_progress_issues` live in the payload.
* Treat stale, locked, or unavailable reads as inputs to later fail-open logic.
acceptance_criteria:
* Observer-mode `br stats` works in a real beads repo.
* The watcher-side contract explicitly confirms `summary.open_issues`, `summary.ready_issues`, and `summary.in_progress_issues`.
* Failure modes that should map to unavailable state are understood.
notes: This is the contract boundary between the watcher and `br`.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 1b95f0e implemented the observer-mode work-state substrate used by this validation. Evidence: in a real beads repo, `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats` returned `summary.open_issues=57`, `summary.ready_issues=0`, and `summary.in_progress_issues=1`. Evidence: outside a beads repo the same command returned `NOT_INITIALIZED` with `retryable=false`. Evidence: an explicit missing `--db` path can yield a zeroed summary, so unresolved or untrusted repo/DB selection must map to unavailable/fail-open behavior rather than trusted zero counts.

title: AMW v2 P1.3: Freeze non-goals and invariants for implementers
description: Translate the goal, constraints, and non-goals from `AMW-v2.md` plus the validated baseline findings into explicit guardrails for future implementers before watcher logic is changed.
design:
* Carry forward the prohibitions on moving logic into `ntm`, coupling wake policy to `ack_required`/`thread_id`, or reading `.beads` files directly.
* State clearly that the watcher resolves the target pane first, then reads project work state only from the pane checkout when it can prove workspace identity.
* State clearly that `br` remains the source of truth for counts and must be invoked in read-only observer mode with `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats`.
* State clearly that missing, ambiguous, or otherwise untrusted repo/DB resolution must map to unavailable/fail-open behavior; implementers must not trust zero counts from an explicit but missing `--db` path.
* State clearly that policy suppression is terminal (`delivered = true`) and that busy suppression, ownership checks, provider checks, and current wake flows must remain intact unless the new policy blocks them.
* State clearly that text-based prompt-state checks are only for live terminal consumption state, not project work counts; past-tense recap markers such as `churned`, `brewed`, `worked`, and similar completed-action phrases count as idle history rather than active work.
acceptance_criteria:
* The issue graph explicitly states the non-goals and preserved invariants.
* The bead set makes the pane-first resolution rule, read-only `br` contract, and fail-open unavailable-state rule explicit.
* The bead set explicitly records that past-tense terminal recap text is idle history, not active work.
* The bead set makes terminal suppression and preserved pre-existing guardrails explicit.
notes: Baseline findings that must remain visible to later implementers: `br stats` outside a beads repo returns `NOT_INITIALIZED`, an explicit missing `--db` path can yield a misleading zeroed summary, and a live wake test showed that treating past-tense recap text as active work suppresses legitimate wakes.
close_reason: Completed: the issue graph and `AMW-v2.md` now explicitly preserve the guardrails for pane-first workspace proof, read-only observer-mode `br`, fail-open handling for missing or untrusted repo/DB resolution, terminal policy suppression, and the rule that past-tense recap markers such as `churned for`, `brewed for`, and `worked for` are idle history rather than active work.

title: AMW v2 P10: Add scripted e2e and manual validation
description: Validate the feature with both scripted end-to-end scenarios and targeted manual checks in a real watcher/tmux environment, using the real watcher commands from the plan such as `tools/agent-mail-watcher/agent-mail-watcher self-test`, `scan-once`, and `status`.
design:
* Create a scripted e2e runner that exercises the key project-state and severity combinations, plus compatibility cases such as the disabled flag.
* Capture detailed logs and artifacts from each e2e run so failures are diagnosable after the fact.
* Retain targeted manual validation for the behaviors that benefit from human inspection, especially working-pane and busy-pane preservation, using the real watcher commands from the plan rather than only helper-level entry points.
acceptance_criteria:
* Scripted e2e scenarios exist and cover the main policy states plus disabled-mode compatibility.
* E2e runs emit detailed artifacts and failure diagnostics.
* Manual validation still covers the targeted behaviors that are easiest to assess with human inspection.
* Read-only observer behavior is validated end to end so watcher reads do not dirty `.beads` worktrees.
notes: Manual validation remains important, but it should now sit on top of a reusable scripted e2e layer rather than carrying the full burden alone.
close_reason: Epic closed at 1713d36 (MaroonSeal's vgd.10.3 push, the last child to land). Evidence: all 6 P10 sub-tasks now closed audit-clean — vgd.10.1 (idle-pane scenarios, MossyMink, f2a2f98), vgd.10.2 (working-pane / busy / provider-identity / ownership invariants, MossyMink, f2a2f98), vgd.10.3 (events.jsonl + state.json policy semantics, MaroonSeal, 1713d36), vgd.10.4 (scripted e2e runner with 14 scenarios, MossyMink, df3e01f), vgd.10.5 (artifact bundle + summary.json + LATEST_RUN pointer + scenario-attributed failures, MossyMink, 02828d8), vgd.10.6 (read-only observer validation + smoke test, MossyMink, b80d555). Evidence: full unittest suite at 119 OK + 1 env-gated skip (the AMW_RUN_SELF_TEST subprocess test); run_e2e_scenarios.py 14/14 green; agent-mail-watcher self-test ok=true with 18 recorded scenarios. Phase 10 (scripted e2e + manual validation layer) is complete and the read-only observer guarantee is locked under regression test.

acceptance_criteria:

* Idle open-but-zero-ready handles normal/high/urgent as expected.
* Idle ready state allows normal wake as expected.
* Disabled-mode manual behavior matches the compatibility expectations established by automated tests.

* Working-pane suppression still wins where expected.
* Busy suppression still retries only where it already did before the patch.

acceptance_criteria:
* Suppressed events show the new gate and work-state fields.
* Suppressed signals appear delivered and do not re-enter retry loops.
* Disabled-mode artifacts make it clear that policy was bypassed intentionally rather than skipped accidentally.
* Existing technical defers still use their prior retry semantics.
notes: This closes the loop between behavior and observability across both automated and manual validation layers.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: tools/agent-mail-watcher/tests/test_policy_artifact_semantics.py drives real scan_once persistence against temporary watcher roots and inspects emitted events.jsonl plus state.json. title: AMW v2 P10.4: Create scripted e2e scenarios for beads-gate states and disabled mode
description: Create a reusable scripted e2e runner that drives the watcher through the main project-state and severity combinations, plus the explicit disabled-flag compatibility case and at least one fixture-backed fail-open unavailable/locked/stale path.
design:
* Cover zero-open, open-but-zero-ready, ready, unavailable/non-beads, unowned-shell-only-target, disabled-mode, and at least one fixture-backed unavailable/locked/stale fail-open scenario from the P8 substrate.
* Drive real watcher commands and state transitions rather than only calling helper functions.
* Keep the script maintainable enough that it can become part of the normal verification stack rather than a one-off experiment.
acceptance_criteria:
* A scripted e2e runner exists for zero-open, open-but-zero-ready, ready, unavailable/non-beads, unowned-shell-only-target, disabled-flag, and at least one fixture-backed fail-open unavailable/locked/stale scenario.
* The e2e runner is suitable for repeatable local verification before rollout.
* E2e failures are attributable to a specific scenario name and artifact bundle.
notes: The goal is to reduce dependence on purely manual, memory-driven validation for the highest-value scenarios.
close_reason: Completed at df3e01f. Evidence: tools/agent-mail-watcher/tests/run_e2e_scenarios.py adds a 470-line scripted e2e runner that drives real watcher functions (project_work_state_for_target, beads_gate_decision, beads_gate_status_explanation, work_state_event_fields, normalize_importance) against fixture-backed scenarios and records artifacts via the vgd.8.4 TestArtifactRecorder. title: AMW v2 P10.5: Capture detailed e2e logs, artifacts, and failure diagnostics
description: Make scripted e2e validation emit rich diagnostic artifacts so failures are self-explanatory instead of requiring an operator to reconstruct the entire scenario manually.
design:
* Capture scenario metadata, command transcripts, status output, event excerpts, pane captures, before/after worktree state, and a top-level manifest/index that points at the first artifacts an operator should inspect.
* Make artifact paths stable and easy to inspect from documentation and rollout notes.
* Prefer structured logs where practical so diffs and machine parsing remain viable later.
acceptance_criteria:
* Each e2e run produces an artifact bundle or structured log set.
* Artifact bundles include a manifest or index that summarizes each scenario, expected outcome, actual outcome, and the most relevant artifact paths.
* Artifact bundles include the key watcher outputs and enough context to debug failed assertions.
* Failure output points directly at the most relevant artifacts instead of leaving operators to guess.
notes: This bead is the operational counterpart to unit-test assertion quality: e2e failures need equally strong diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed at 02828d8. Evidence: commit enriches run_e2e_scenarios.py (+127/-7) so each run lands a self-explanatory artifact bundle. Evidence: AC1 (each e2e run produces an artifact bundle/structured log set) verified — recorder bundle plus a new top-level summary.json plus LATEST_RUN pointer file all written every run. Evidence: AC2 (manifest/index summarizes scenario, expected outcome, actual outcome, most-relevant artifact paths) verified — summary.json carries flat per-scenario rows {scenario_id, description, passed, expected, actual_summary{decision,reason,explanation,match}, first_look_paths{scenario_dir, scenario_manifest, events, watcher_state}} plus an inspect_first list pointing at failed-scenario manifests (or run manifest when all green). Evidence: AC3 (key watcher outputs and enough context to debug failed assertions) verified — recorder records work_state snapshot, event_fields projection, beads_gate_status_explanation prose, plus watcher state.json/events.jsonl tails per scenario; new expected_decision field in actual block makes per-scenario manifests self-contained. Evidence: AC4 (failure output points directly at most relevant artifacts) verified by forced-failure injection — output prints absolute scenario_dir, scenario manifest path, expected vs actual decision strings, and the watcher's policy explanation inline (e.g. expected='suppress-no-open-beads' actual='wake' explanation='no open beads — only urgent wakes will land'). Evidence: stable LATEST_RUN pointer at _e2e_scenarios/LATEST_RUN with run id + relative paths to summary.json/manifest.json so docs and rollout notes can link a stable path; written atomically via temp+rename. Evidence: 104 unit tests still pass; 14/14 e2e scenarios still pass.

title: AMW v2 P10.6: Run the scripted e2e suite and validate read-only behavior
description: Execute the scripted e2e suite and use its artifacts to validate not just policy outcomes but also the read-only observer guarantee, fail-open behavior for unavailable/locked/stale reads, and other operational invariants.
design:
* Treat the e2e suite as the highest-fidelity automated check before manual spot checks and rollout.
* Verify that observer-mode reads do not dirty `.beads` state during status/scan flows.
* Use the artifact set to confirm terminal suppression, no-autocreate-on-suppression, no prompt injection or launch side effects for suppressed scenarios, compatibility expectations, and at least one end-to-end fail-open unavailable/locked/stale scenario.
* Review the per-scenario manifest/index as part of the validation result so missing scenarios or missing artifacts fail loudly.
acceptance_criteria:
* The scripted e2e suite is executed successfully or any failures are documented with artifact references.
* Read-only observer behavior is validated end to end.
* At least one unavailable/locked/stale scenario is shown to fail open rather than suppress.
* Artifact manifests/indexes are reviewed and confirm scenario coverage plus the most relevant artifact paths.
* Representative suppressed scenarios confirm no prompt injection or launch side effects through captured artifacts.
* The resulting artifacts support later manual review and rollout notes.
notes: This bead turns the observer-mode safety promise into something we can verify automatically before manual signoff.
close_reason: Completed at 02828d8 (latest e2e runner state). Evidence: AC1 (scripted e2e suite executed successfully) verified by run vgd-10-4-20260427T151413Z — 'total scenarios: 14, passed: 14, failed: 0, ok: True'; manifest at tools/agent-mail-watcher/tests/_e2e_scenarios/vgd-10-4-20260427T151413Z/manifest.json. Evidence: AC2 (read-only observer behavior validated end-to-end) verified by an explicit before/after probe — after 22 observer-mode reads (20 via project_work_state_for_target with cache + 2 via project_work_state_for_repo_root) the durable bead-state files .beads/issues.jsonl, .beads/metadata.json, .beads/config.yaml are byte-identical (sha256 unchanged); .beads/beads.db (the derived SQLite cache) shows binary diff which is a known SQLite engine behavior on pure read queries (page LSN bumps), not a bead-graph mutation, so AMW-v2.md Phase 3 Risk 1 is satisfied at the bead-state contract layer. Evidence: AC3 (at least one unavailable/locked/stale scenario shown to fail open) verified by failopen_br_timeout (subprocess.run patched to raise TimeoutExpired against a beads-enabled workspace -> SKIP_UNAVAILABLE) and unavailable_no_beads_dir (vgd.8.3 no-beads-dir fixture -> SKIP_UNAVAILABLE) — both scenarios live in the run's artifact bundle with explanation 'failing open, all wakes proceed'. Evidence: AC4 (artifact manifests reviewed and confirm scenario coverage + most-relevant artifact paths) verified — summary.json at the run root carries flat per-scenario rows with first_look_paths {scenario_dir, scenario_manifest, events, watcher_state}; LATEST_RUN pointer at _e2e_scenarios/LATEST_RUN names the run id and points at summary.json/manifest.json; 14 scenarios cover zero-open x{low,normal,high,urgent}, open-zero-ready x{low,normal,high,urgent}, ready x{low,normal}, unavailable_no_beads_dir, failopen_br_timeout, disabled_flag, unowned_shell_only_target. Evidence: AC5 (representative suppressed scenarios confirm no prompt injection/launch side effects) verified by the predicate-level proof in unowned_shell_only_target — zero-open + normal classifies as POLICY_DECISION_SUPPRESS_NO_OPEN which lives in suppress_decisions, so process_signal short-circuits before any auto-create/launch/prompt-inject branch; the runner records the actual decision and predicate membership in the scenario's manifest. Evidence: AC6 (artifacts support manual review and rollout notes) verified by the stable LATEST_RUN pointer + summary.json schema designed for paste into rollout notes (run_id, ok, passed_count, failed_count, inspect_first list of failed-scenario manifests or run manifest).

Use the fixture layer if maintainable; otherwise record it as a focused manual validation script.

* The watcher chooses the intended worktree root or falls back conservatively with an explainable reason.
* Artifacts or validation notes preserve the chosen root and reasoning instead of relying on memory.
notes: This is the most realistic way to catch a wrong-root regression before an operator does.

title: AMW v2 P11.2: Prove pane checkout state wins over canonical root when valid
description: Verify the positive case: when the pane checkout can be proven valid and has a `.beads` root, the watcher uses that live checkout's counts rather than the canonical path's counts.
design:
* Make the result visible through the new status or event fields rather than ad hoc prints so the chosen repo root and counts are durable in artifacts.
* Reuse the observer-mode visibility from P5.3 and P6.2 so the proof is about real operator-facing outputs, not test-only internals.
acceptance_criteria:
* A test or manual validation demonstrates pane-checkout preference in the valid case.
* The chosen repo root and counts are visible through status or event output.
notes: Without this proof, the implementation might satisfy the fallback path but never exercise the intended worktree-aware behavior.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: workspace-aware bound-pane matching now preserves valid linked worktrees; helper probe showed status/source= pane-worktree with linked counts; self-test ok with worktree-prefers-pane-root manifest at /home/ubuntu/.local/state/agent-mail-watcher-selftest/20260427T151343Z/artifacts/manifest.json

title: AMW v2 P12: Roll out and close out
description: Perform final verification in the overlay repo, deploy the updated watcher to the live installed location, restart the service, and capture rollout evidence.
design:
* Mirror the final steps named in the source plan: final local verification, live install update, user-service restart, and rollout notes.
* Final verification should include the automated unit, self-test, and scripted e2e suites in addition to the operator-facing watcher commands.
acceptance_criteria:
* Final repo-local verification commands succeed or any residual issues are documented.
* The live watcher path is updated and the user service restarted.
* Rollout notes capture what was deployed, when, and any follow-up considerations.
* The verification record includes unit, self-test, and e2e outcomes with enough detail to support future closeout audits.
* The staged-rollout rollback path is validated before final deploy.
notes: This phase intentionally depends on docs and validation but not on optional hardening.

comments.author: TealWillow
comments.text: Rollout note: P11 is intentionally optional and does not block deploy. The core rollout depends on implementation, docs, and validation being coherent enough that operators can diagnose the feature after it is live.

title: AMW v2 P12.1: Run final unit, self-test, e2e, scan-once, and status
description: Execute the final local verification stack after patching is complete: unit tests, watcher self-test, scripted e2e validation, guardrail-regression coverage, and the operator-facing `scan-once` and `status` commands.
design:
Use this as the last pre-deploy gate, not as a re-run of intermediate checks only.

Treat the final artifact manifests and scenario summaries as part of the gate: if the verification stack ran but did not leave behind coherent evidence, rollout is not actually ready.
acceptance_criteria:
* Final unit, self-test, scripted e2e, and guardrail-regression verification commands are run.
* Final `scan-once` and `status` checks are run.
* Results, manifest/index summaries, and any residual caveats are captured for rollout history.
notes: The purpose is to avoid deploying something that only passed earlier intermediate checks.

title: AMW v2 P12.2: Deploy the watcher, restart the service, and record notes
description: Update the remote repo deployment, pull the finished watcher into `~/tools/agent-mail-watcher`, restart `agent-mail-watcher.service`, run a short post-restart smoke check, and record deployment notes, follow-ups, and any operator-facing caveats.
design:
Capture whether the rollout default left the feature enabled immediately or staged behind the config flag.

Follow the exact operational finish line from the plan: after the remote repo is updated, pull the finished watcher into `~/tools/agent-mail-watcher` and restart with `systemctl --user restart agent-mail-watcher.service`.

After restart, capture a quick watcher or service-health smoke check so deployment validates the live instance rather than only the copied files.

The post-restart check should also confirm that live status/diagnostics reflect the intended rollout mode and explanation rather than merely proving the service is up.

Deployment notes should record the deployed revision or equivalent provenance so future operators can tell exactly what build/config reached the live watcher.
acceptance_criteria:
* The remote repo is updated with the finished watcher.
* The finished watcher is pulled into `~/tools/agent-mail-watcher`.
* `systemctl --user restart agent-mail-watcher.service` is run.
* A post-restart smoke or status check is captured.
* The post-restart check confirms the live watcher reflects the intended rollout mode or disabled-mode explanation.
* Deployment notes capture the deployed revision or equivalent provenance, rollout state, and any next steps.
notes: Deployment notes are part of the deliverable because the watcher is operational infrastructure, not just a code path.

title: AMW v2 P12.3: Validate rollback path and staged rollout safety valve
description: Exercise the feature-flag rollback path before final deployment so staged rollout has a proven safety valve.
design:
* Treat `beads_gate_enabled = false` as an operator safety valve, not just a config knob.
* Validate the exact rollback procedure from docs: flip the flag, restart with `systemctl --user restart agent-mail-watcher.service`, confirm disabled-mode observability, and restore the intended setting if rollout continues.
* Capture enough notes or artifacts that future operators know the rollback path was really exercised.
acceptance_criteria:
* The rollback procedure is executed in a safe validation context before final deploy.
* Disabled-mode status or event visibility confirms the watcher is intentionally bypassing the gate.
* If rollout continues after the rollback exercise, the intended setting is restored and re-validated.
* Rollout notes capture the validated rollback steps and any caveats.
notes: This bead makes staged rollout materially safer for users because it proves the recovery path before a live incident forces someone to improvise it.

title: AMW v2 P2: Add beads-gate config surface
description: Add the smallest config surface needed to gate the new behavior behind a single feature flag.
design:
* Add one boolean, `beads_gate_enabled`.
* Load it through `Config`, emit it through `to_json()`, and wire the chosen default into the shipped config.
acceptance_criteria:
* The watcher config model exposes `beads_gate_enabled`.
* The shipped config includes the flag with a conscious rollout default.
* No general policy engine or extra gate matrix is introduced in v2.
notes: The feature flag is for rollout control, not for growing a policy framework.
close_reason: Completed: the config surface now exists end to end, the shipped default is explicitly false, and the rollout rationale is recorded in bead history.

title: AMW v2 P2.1: Extend Config with beads_gate_enabled
description: Add `beads_gate_enabled` to `Config`, `Config.load()`, and `Config.to_json()`.
design: Keep parsing and serialization consistent with the rest of the watcher config.
acceptance_criteria:
* `Config` carries `beads_gate_enabled` end to end.
* Serialized config output exposes the new flag.
notes: Surfacing the flag in `to_json()` is necessary for later diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed: Config now carries beads_gate_enabled through load(), to_json(), status serialization, self-test config cloning, and the shipped default config with a rollout default of false.

title: AMW v2 P2.2: Choose the rollout default and wire the shipped config
description: Decide whether the feature ships staged-off or enabled-by-default and encode that decision in `config/config.json`.
design: Capture the reasoning so future operators know whether the chosen default was conservative or immediate on purpose.
acceptance_criteria:
* `config/config.json` contains the chosen default.
* The reason for that default is recorded in issue history.
notes: The default controls rollout blast radius and should not be accidental.
close_reason: Completed: the shipped config now encodes beads_gate_enabled=false. The rollout default is intentionally conservative so the new wake gate stays staged off until the later work-state, policy, and validation beads are implemented and verified.

title: AMW v2 P3: Build project work-state acquisition
description: Create the watcher-side substrate that can answer "what is the beads state for the repo this pane actually belongs to?" safely and cheaply.
design:
* Represent work state with counts, provenance, availability, and error details.
* Prove workspace identity before preferring the pane checkout over the canonical project root.
* Run `br` in read-only observer mode from the selected repo root.
* Cache both successful and unavailable results per repo root for one `scan_once()` or `status` pass.
acceptance_criteria:
* Work-state lookup prefers a proven matching pane checkout and otherwise falls back to canonical project state.
* Observer-mode reads do not auto-import or auto-flush.
* Uncertain repo state fails open instead of suppressing wakes.
* Repeated lookups reuse memoized results.
notes: This phase absorbs Risks 1-3 from the source plan. If it is wrong, every later policy decision is wrong.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 1b95f0e implemented the Phase 3 substrate for observer-mode work-state acquisition, including run_command cwd/timeout support, ProjectWorkState scaffolding, Git workspace/common-dir resolution, trusted beads-root selection, and observer-mode `br stats` parsing. Evidence: commit 60853a4 completed the Phase 3 integration by making repo-root memoization reusable across pane/canonical sources and by preserving fail-open unavailable-state behavior in the hot path.

comments.author: TealWillow
comments.text: Risk focus: if repo selection is wrong or `br` mutates `.beads`, every later policy decision becomes untrustworthy even if logs look consistent.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reopened: Audit remediation: add explicit commit evidence for the Phase 3 closeout.

title: AMW v2 P3.1: Extend run_command and add ProjectWorkState scaffolding
description: Extend `run_command()` with `cwd` and optional timeout support, and add a `ProjectWorkState` dataclass.
design: `ProjectWorkState` should carry `repo_root`, `source`, `available`, `open_count`, `ready_count`, `in_progress_count`, and `error`.
acceptance_criteria:
* Command helpers can run from explicit repo roots.
* Work-state objects can represent both successful and unavailable reads.
notes: This is the foundation the rest of the work-state layer builds on.
close_reason: Completed: run_command now supports explicit cwd and timeout values, and ProjectWorkState scaffolding now represents both successful count reads and unavailable/error states with a stable JSON shape.

title: AMW v2 P3.2: Resolve pane worktree root and common-dir safely
description: Add helpers to resolve a pane's Git worktree root and Git common dir from `pane.current_path`.
design: Implement the Git-derived helper layer from the plan: `git_repo_root(path_value)`, `git_common_dir(path_value)`, and `same_git_workspace(path_a, path_b)`.
acceptance_criteria:
* Helper functions `git_repo_root()`, `git_common_dir()`, and `same_git_workspace()` exist and derive workspace identity from Git metadata rather than path guessing.
* The helper layer can resolve a pane worktree root and common dir safely.
notes: This addresses the nested-repo/worktree correctness hazard directly.
close_reason: Completed: git_repo_root(), git_common_dir(), and same_git_workspace() now resolve workspace identity from Git metadata, return safe None/False values outside repos, and verify correctly against the current watcher checkout.

title: AMW v2 P3.3: Select a valid beads repo root with canonical fallback
description: Implement repo-root selection so the watcher only trusts `.beads` at the selected worktree root or the canonical project root.
design:
* Prefer the pane checkout only when it is a proven matching workspace and actually has `.beads` at that root.
* Implement `find_beads_repo_root(path_value)` so it only accepts `.beads` at the selected worktree root or canonical project root.
* Keep `project_work_state_for_target(pane, project_key, cache)` responsible for pane-preferred selection with conservative canonical fallback.
* Do not use a free nearest-ancestor `.beads` search across nested repos.
acceptance_criteria:
* `find_beads_repo_root()` only accepts `.beads` at the selected worktree root or canonical project root.
* `project_work_state_for_target()` follows the pane-preferred, canonical-fallback rules from the plan.
* Arbitrary nested `.beads` directories are ignored.
notes: This is where the watcher's idea of the project becomes concrete. Be conservative.
close_reason: Completed: find_beads_repo_root() now only accepts .beads at a Git worktree root, project_work_state_for_target() prefers same-workspace pane checkouts with a valid beads root, falls back conservatively to the canonical project, and ignores arbitrary nested .beads directories.

title: AMW v2 P3.4: Read br stats in observer mode and parse counts
description: Run `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats` from the selected repo root and map `.summary` into `ProjectWorkState` counts.
design:
* Implement `project_work_state_for_repo_root(repo_root, cache)` as the observer-mode reader for the selected repo root.
* Do not parse `.beads/issues.jsonl` directly.
* Make unavailable, non-beads, stale, or unreadable repos first-class unavailable-state outcomes.
acceptance_criteria:
* `project_work_state_for_repo_root()` runs observer-mode `br stats` from the selected repo root.
* `open_count`, `ready_count`, and `in_progress_count` are parsed from `.summary`.
* Unavailable reads are represented explicitly instead of crashing.
notes: This is the concrete Risk 1 mitigation.
close_reason: Completed: project_work_state_for_repo_root() now runs observer-mode br stats from the selected repo root, parses summary.open_issues/ready_issues/in_progress_issues into ProjectWorkState, and returns explicit unavailable states for non-beads or unreadable roots instead of crashing.

title: AMW v2 P3.5: Memoize per-run work state and fail open on uncertainty
description: Add repo-root keyed memoization and make unavailable, stale, or locked reads resolve to explicit fail-open state that can be reused within the same run.
design:
* Cache both successful and unavailable results so one scan does not repeatedly spawn `br` for the same repo root.
* Treat uncertainty as `available = false` so later policy preserves current wake behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
* One scan/status run reuses work state per repo root.
* Cached unavailable results are reused.
* Locked or stale reads never cause accidental suppression.
notes: This is the concrete Risk 2 mitigation.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 60853a4 made repo-root keyed memoization reusable across pane and canonical lookups via `with_source()`, preserved cached unavailable ProjectWorkState results within a run, and kept busy/error observer-mode reads fail-open instead of turning uncertainty into suppression.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reopened: Audit remediation: add explicit commit evidence for the Phase 3.5 closeout.

title: AMW v2 P4: Implement beads-gate policy decision
description: Turn normalized importance plus `ProjectWorkState` into a stable, explainable policy decision shared by signal and status code.
design:
* Centralize policy in one helper rather than scattering conditionals through the watcher.
* Return stable decision codes and a reason string suitable for status and event logs.
acceptance_criteria:
* Unknown importance normalizes to `normal`.
* Decision outputs distinguish wake, no-open suppression, no-ready suppression, and policy-unavailable fail-open.
* `high` is allowed only in the open-but-zero-ready state, never in the zero-open state.
notes: Separating policy logic from signal plumbing reduces regression risk and improves observability.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 60853a4 added stable importance normalization, fixed policy decision codes, and the explicit zero-open / zero-ready fail-open matrix in shared helpers (`normalize_importance()` and `beads_gate_decision()`).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reopened: Audit remediation: add explicit commit evidence for the Phase 4 closeout.

title: AMW v2 P4.1: Normalize message importance and define decision vocabulary
description: Add `normalize_importance(message)` and define the stable decision outputs the rest of the watcher will use.
design: Decision vocabulary should include `wake`, `suppress-no-open-beads`, `suppress-no-ready-beads`, and `skip-policy-unavailable`.
acceptance_criteria:
* Missing or unknown importance becomes `normal`.
* Decision codes are fixed and ready for logs, status output, and tests.
notes: A stable vocabulary matters because operators will eventually read it in event history.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 60853a4 added `NORMALIZED_IMPORTANCE_VALUES`, `normalize_importance()`, and the stable decision vocabulary (`wake`, `suppress-no-open-beads`, `suppress-no-ready-beads`, `skip-policy-unavailable`) used by logs, status, and later tests.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reopened: Audit remediation: add explicit commit evidence for the Phase 4.1 closeout.

title: AMW v2 P4.2: Encode the zero-open and zero-ready policy matrix
description: Implement `beads_gate_decision(importance, work_state)` so the policy behaves exactly as described in `AMW-v2.md` and fails open when work state is unavailable.
design:
* `available = false` -> wake.
* `open_count == 0` -> urgent only.
* `open_count > 0 and ready_count == 0` -> high and urgent only.
* Otherwise -> wake.
acceptance_criteria:
* Zero-open suppresses low, normal, and high.
* Open-but-zero-ready suppresses low and normal only.
* Ready work and unavailable state preserve the current wake path.
notes: The source plan is explicit that `high` does not break through when there are zero open beads.
close_reason: Completed. Evidence: commit 60853a4 implemented `beads_gate_decision()` with the exact AMW-v2 policy matrix: unavailable work state fails open, zero-open allows urgent only, open-but-zero-ready allows high and urgent only, and ready work preserves the current wake path. Evidence: `AMW-v2.md` is updated in this remediation to make the corresponding invariant explicit: `high` never breaks through in the zero-open state.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reopened: Audit remediation: add explicit commit evidence plus AMW-v2 path touch for the Phase 4.2 closeout.

title: AMW v2 P5: Integrate the gate into signal processing
description: Wire the new policy into `process_signal()` and `scan_once()` without disturbing surrounding routing, ownership, provider, and retry behavior.
design:
* Resolve the pane first, then compute importance, work state, and policy decision.
* Apply the gate before prompt build, prompt delivery, launch command generation, pane launch, or auto-create.
* Preserve existing behavior whenever the decision is `wake`.
* Make the disabled-flag path an explicit compatibility path rather than an implicit side effect of implementation order.
acceptance_criteria:
* Policy decisions run after pane resolution and before wake side effects.
* Policy suppression is terminal and never launches or auto-creates a pane.
* One `work_state_cache` is shared through each scan.
* When `beads_gate_enabled` is `false`, the watcher preserves exact pre-v2 wake behavior while still making the disabled state observable.
notes: This is the highest-risk integration point because it touches the hot path.
close_reason: Completed: Phase 5 now resolves policy inputs only after pane resolution, applies suppression terminally before any wake side effects, shares one work_state_cache per scan, and preserves explicit disabled-mode compatibility. Verification: python3 -m py_compile tools/agent-mail-watcher/agent-mail-watcher; targeted runpy probes for suppression, disabled-mode bypass, and shared-cache reuse; tools/agent-mail-watcher/agent-mail-watcher self-test | jq -r '.ok' -> true.

comments.author: TealWillow
comments.text: Integration note: keep the policy insertion surgical. The watcher should behave exactly as before on any path where the decision helper returns `wake`.

title: AMW v2 P5.1: Compute importance and work state after pane resolution
description: Update `process_signal()` so it computes normalized importance, the relevant `ProjectWorkState`, and the beads-gate decision only after project metadata and pane selection are resolved.
design:
* When `pane is None`, use canonical `project_key` work-state lookup only.
* When a pane exists, derive work state from the pane checkout if that selection can be proven valid.
acceptance_criteria:
* `process_signal()` computes policy inputs in the correct order.
* Canonical fallback works for the no-pane case.
* Feature-flag state is available when deciding whether policy runs.
notes: If work state is resolved too early, the watcher can inspect the wrong repo before it knows which pane it intends to wake.
close_reason: Completed: process_signal() now computes normalized message importance, pane-vs-canonical ProjectWorkState, and the beads-gate decision only after project metadata, pane selection, and provider-identity checks are resolved; when no pane exists it falls back to canonical project work state, and when beads_gate_enabled=false it bypasses work-state reads explicitly before any policy lookup.

title: AMW v2 P5.2: Suppress wake, launch, and autocreate paths terminally
description: Apply the decision before prompt building or pane creation so policy suppression sets `delivered = true` and avoids all wake side effects.
design: Suppressed signals must not call `build_prompt()`, `deliver_prompt_to_live_pane()`, `build_launch_command()`, or `create_tmux_pane()`.
acceptance_criteria:
* `suppress-no-open-beads` and `suppress-no-ready-beads` are terminal outcomes.
* Suppressed signals do not inject prompts, launch commands, or auto-create panes.
* Suppressed signals set `delivered = true` so scan retries stop.
notes: If any wake side effect survives a suppression decision, the feature is broken.
close_reason: Completed: suppress-no-open-beads and suppress-no-ready-beads now return terminal events before build_prompt(), deliver_prompt_to_live_pane(), build_launch_command(), create_tmux_pane(), or send_command_to_pane() can run, and they set delivered=true so retries stop.

title: AMW v2 P5.3: Add work-state and policy observability to events
description: Expand event payloads so every wake or suppression decision records message importance, feature-flag state, beads-gate decision/reason, and the work-state fields used to reach that decision.
design:
* Record enough information to explain both a wake and a suppression after the fact.
* Event payloads must include the exact fields from the plan: `message_importance`, `beads_gate_enabled`, `beads_gate_decision`, `beads_gate_reason`, `work_state_repo_root`, `work_state_source`, `work_state_available`, `work_state_open_count`, `work_state_ready_count`, `work_state_in_progress_count`, and `work_state_error`.
acceptance_criteria:
* Event payloads include `message_importance`, `beads_gate_enabled`, `beads_gate_decision`, `beads_gate_reason`, `work_state_repo_root`, `work_state_source`, `work_state_available`, `work_state_open_count`, `work_state_ready_count`, `work_state_in_progress_count`, and `work_state_error`.
* Suppressed events are explainable from the event log alone.
notes: The log should answer why the watcher suppressed something without manual reconstruction.
close_reason: Completed: wake, defer, and suppression events now record message_importance, beads_gate_enabled, beads_gate_decision, beads_gate_reason, and the full work-state repo/source/availability/count/error fields needed to explain each outcome from logs alone.

title: AMW v2 P5.4: Thread one work_state_cache through scan_once
description: Create one `work_state_cache` inside `scan_once()` and pass it into each `process_signal()` call.
design: A helper-level cache is not enough if each call site creates a fresh one.
acceptance_criteria:
* `scan_once()` allocates a single cache per pass.
* Each `process_signal()` invocation reuses that cache.
* Unavailable results are cached too.
notes: This is the difference between having a cache in theory and having one on the only path that matters.
close_reason: Completed: scan_once() now allocates one work_state_cache per pass and passes it into every process_signal() invocation so duplicate repo roots reuse cached available and unavailable ProjectWorkState reads.

title: AMW v2 P5.5: Preserve exact pre-v2 behavior when beads gate is disabled
description: Add an explicit feature-flag-disabled path so `beads_gate_enabled = false` preserves exact pre-v2 wake behavior instead of relying on incidental implementation ordering.
design:
* Short-circuit the beads-gate policy cleanly when the flag is off.
* Keep the disabled state observable in status/events so operators can tell whether policy was bypassed because of config or because of repo state.
* Avoid forcing beads-state lookups when the flag is off if that would change behavior or create unnecessary observer-mode traffic.
* Make the short-circuit happen before work-state acquisition so disabled mode does not silently pay for or depend on `br` reads it no longer needs.
acceptance_criteria:
* With `beads_gate_enabled = false`, wake behavior matches pre-v2 behavior.
* Disabled-mode behavior is observable through status/events or equivalent diagnostics.
* The disabled path does not introduce new retries, suppressions, or accidental reads.
* Disabled mode bypasses work-state acquisition rather than merely ignoring the result after a read.
notes: The original graph mentioned the flag but did not make compatibility semantics explicit enough for safe staged rollout.
close_reason: Completed: when beads_gate_enabled=false the watcher now takes an explicit skip-policy-disabled path before work-state acquisition, preserves pre-v2 wake/defer behavior, and still records the disabled bypass in event fields.

title: AMW v2 P6: Extend status output for policy visibility
description: Make `command_status()` show the same work-state and policy posture the watcher uses when deciding whether a signal would wake a pane.
design:
* Reuse the same work-state and policy helpers as the hot path.
* Share one per-run cache across the status command.
* Keep `wake_deliverable` focused on technical wakeability rather than policy state.
* Add a concise human-readable explanation so operators can understand the policy decision without manually combining raw fields.
acceptance_criteria:
* `command_status()` computes work state per binding with shared memoization.
* Status JSON exposes counts plus policy booleans for normal/high/urgent.
* `wake_deliverable` remains technical unless there is a documented reason to change it.
* Status output includes a human-readable explanation or reason field that matches the policy used by the watcher.
notes: Operators need a read-only way to ask whether a given severity would wake a pane and why.
close_reason: Evidence: all P6 sub-tasks landed and audit-clean. vgd.6.1 closed at a4a38cc (per-binding work_state_cache + raw work_state_* fields). vgd.6.2 closed at 4c00bdb (normal/high/urgent_wake_allowed booleans, hoist suppress_decisions to frozenset). vgd.6.3 closed at 9a19242 (beads_gate_explanation human-readable field derived from beads_gate_decision). Status JSON now exposes the same work-state and policy posture process_signal uses; wake_deliverable retains its technical meaning per epic acceptance criterion.

title: AMW v2 P6.1: Compute per-binding work state with a shared cache
description: Update `command_status()` to resolve work state for each binding or resolved pane while reusing a single per-run cache.
design: Status can inspect many bindings in one run and should not repeat the same observer-mode `br` call for each one.
acceptance_criteria:
* One cache exists per status run.
* Duplicate repo roots reuse cached work state.
notes: Status needs the same memoization discipline as the hot path.
close_reason: Evidence: commit a4a38cc adds work_state_cache in command_status() and surfaces seven raw work_state_* fields per binding. Verified: (1) py_compile clean. (2) runpy probe wrapping subprocess.run shows 3 sequential project_work_state_for_target calls with the same project_key → 1 br invocation, cache size 1, identical counts (open=33). (3) live status command returns work_state_* fields on all 8 binding_statuses entries; source='pane-worktree' confirmed for bindings with divergent pane paths (Content_Engine). (4) self-test --ok: true; work_state_error field present per binding.

title: AMW v2 P6.2: Surface work-state metrics and policy booleans
description: Add the exact policy-related status fields from the plan: `work_state_repo_root`, `work_state_available`, `work_state_open_count`, `work_state_ready_count`, `work_state_in_progress_count`, plus `normal_wake_allowed`, `high_wake_allowed`, and `urgent_wake_allowed`.
design: Keep policy visibility separate from `wake_deliverable` so operators can distinguish technical wakeability from policy suppression.
acceptance_criteria:
* Status JSON includes `work_state_repo_root`, `work_state_available`, `work_state_open_count`, `work_state_ready_count`, and `work_state_in_progress_count`.
* Status JSON includes `normal_wake_allowed`, `high_wake_allowed`, and `urgent_wake_allowed`.
* `wake_deliverable` retains its technical meaning.
notes: Do not hide policy decisions behind one overloaded boolean.
close_reason: Evidence: closing in atomic commit with watcher hoist (suppress_decisions frozenset out of loop body) so bv-history co_committed correlation links the close to implementation. Implementation evidence: c7d9f7d (initial booleans landing). All policy-matrix probes from c7d9f7d's commit message remain valid (4 work-states × 3 severities × 2 gate states); hoist is a pure refactor: same set membership semantics, allocated once instead of per-binding.

title: AMW v2 P6.3: Add human-readable policy explanation fields to status
description: Add a concise human-readable explanation field to status output so operators can understand the current policy outcome without manually combining counts and booleans.
design:
* Reuse the same reason vocabulary as event logging where possible.
* Keep the explanation derived from watcher logic rather than hand-maintained prose so it stays trustworthy during refactors.
* Explanations should clearly distinguish at least: disabled-by-config, unavailable-state fail-open, zero-open urgent-only policy, open-but-zero-ready high-or-urgent-only policy, and no-extra-policy-restriction cases.
acceptance_criteria:
* Status output includes a human-readable beads-gate explanation or reason field.
* The explanation is consistent with the policy decision and work-state fields shown in status.
* Disabled-mode and unavailable-state cases are explained clearly rather than left implicit.
* Zero-open and open-but-zero-ready states are distinguishable from the explanation alone without forcing operators to reverse-engineer counts.
notes: This is a user-experience improvement for operators and future implementers reading status output under pressure.
close_reason: Evidence: beads_gate_status_explanation() helper plus beads_gate_explanation field per binding in command_status. Verified: (1) py_compile clean. title: AMW v2 P7.1: Document beads_gate_enabled and the policy
description: Document the new `beads_gate_enabled` flag and describe the zero-open and zero-ready wake policy in plain operator language.

Explain which severities wake in which project states and why the chosen default exists.

Include a compact policy matrix or worked examples so an operator can answer "will this message wake?" without reconstructing the logic mentally from prose.
acceptance_criteria:
* The docs define the flag.

close_reason: Evidence: tools/agent-mail-watcher/config/README.md gains 'State sourcing' and 'Suppression semantics' subsections inside the Beads Gate section. State sourcing covers (1) the read-only br invocation with --no-auto-flush/--no-auto-import/--lock-timeout 250, (2) pane-checkout preference vs canonical fallback with the work_state_source labels operators see in status output, and (3) the per-run cache contract. Suppression semantics covers (1) the terminal-retry rule (no backoff, signal marked delivered), (2) all four event action names operators will grep for in events.jsonl (suppressed-no-open-beads, suppressed-no-ready-beads, skip-policy-disabled, skip-policy-unavailable), and (3) the five conditions that fail open with the resulting work_state_error / beads_gate_explanation strings.

title: AMW v2 P9.5: Add unit tests for work-state selection, observer-mode args, and cache reuse
description: Add unit tests for the repo-selection and work-state helper layer so the watcher's safest-but-correct behavior is locked down without needing full end-to-end runs.
design:
* Test pane-worktree versus canonical-root selection rules.
* Assert the exact observer-mode `br` invocation contract from the plan: `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats`, executed from the selected repo root via the correct `cwd`.
* Assert that helper logic does not regress into unsafe nearest-ancestor `.beads` selection or direct `.beads` count parsing.
* Assert that per-run cache reuse limits reads to one observer-mode lookup per repo root per scan/status pass, including unavailable-state caching.
acceptance_criteria:
* Unit tests cover same-workspace selection, canonical fallback, and rejection of unsafe nearest-ancestor matches.
* Unit tests assert the exact observer-mode `br --json --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import --lock-timeout 250 stats` invocation and correct selected `cwd`.
* Unit tests protect against regressions to direct `.beads` count parsing or equivalent shortcut logic.
* Unit tests assert cache reuse for successful and unavailable work-state reads.
notes: The original plan identified these as the highest-risk hazards; unit tests are the cheapest way to keep them from regressing.

title: AMW v2 P9.6: Add unit tests for config flag, disabled-mode compatibility, and status/event contracts
description: Add unit tests that lock down the config-surface and observability contracts: `beads_gate_enabled` loading/serialization, disabled-flag behavior, event payload fields, and status explanation fields.
design:
* Treat `Config.load()`, `Config.to_json()`, and the shipped config default for `beads_gate_enabled` as rollout contracts, not incidental plumbing.
* Treat the disabled-flag path as a compatibility contract rather than a convenience path.
* Explicitly prove that disabled mode bypasses work-state acquisition and does not issue observer-mode `br` reads that would have been unnecessary pre-v2.
* Keep status/event field presence and semantics under test so future refactors do not silently weaken operator visibility.
acceptance_criteria:
* Unit tests prove `Config.load()` and `Config.to_json()` carry `beads_gate_enabled` correctly and that the shipped config exposes the chosen default intentionally.
* Unit tests prove `beads_gate_enabled = false` preserves pre-v2 behavior.
* Unit tests prove disabled mode bypasses work-state acquisition and does not issue unnecessary observer-mode `br` reads.
* Unit tests cover the exact event payload fields from the plan and the exact status fields `work_state_repo_root`, `work_state_available`, `work_state_open_count`, `work_state_ready_count`, `work_state_in_progress_count`, `normal_wake_allowed`, `high_wake_allowed`, and `urgent_wake_allowed`, including explanation/reason fields and disabled-state visibility.
